entry.title: EUR. 1 certificate printed for undefined preference status of item in export declaration
entry.content:
Hello,

We are printing EUR. 1 certificate with message P0050 in export declaration from Germany, in SAP GTS E4H.

Problem is, the EUR. 1 certificate is printed with a single item if this item has preference status 'undefined' and is the only one item in the export declaration.

For example for an export from Germany to USA, the one and only one item in the export declaration, has preference status 'undefined' and this item is printed on the EUR.1 certificate.

How to suppress printing of message P0050 for countries with no preference agreements?

Any input are highly appreciated. 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Integrating SII for Spain with SAP DRC
entry.content:
Hello!

The error “You need to define the result for process ESSIIBUN step DRCC_RESP first (EDOCPROCSETFLAG)” does not mean that inbound messages from the tax authority are missing from the communication setup. The step DRCC_RESP represents the response coming back from the tax authority, SAP cannot continue processing because the step result, status mapping is missing.

When the SII system sends a response back, SAP receives it, but doesn’t know which eDocument status to set, and therefore display this error.

The missing configuration is in the eDocument for:

  • Process: ESSIIBUN
  • Process step: DRCC_RESP

You need to define at least one step result and map it to a document status using process flags (EDOCPROCSETFLAG).

 

Below you can find usefull documentation about this process:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/439a34c3d6ec406a91fb29585ab0ede5/4f26530c78ff4904a960e5d475791e2d.html?locale=en-US&q=DRCC_RESP

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/439a34c3d6ec406a91fb29585ab0ede5/4f26530c78ff4904a960e5d475791e2d.html?locale=en-US&q=DRCC_RESP

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/439a34c3d6ec406a91fb29585ab0ede5/4f26530c78ff4904a960e5d475791e2d.html?locale=en-US&q=DRCC_RESP

 

In addition a similar error message is explained in this OSS note for Poland:

3727482 - error "you need to define the result for process plksef step drcc_resp first (edoprocsetflag)" edocument064" while DRC Poland Invoice submission

entry.title: Re: We need to delete Serializing procedures (POSL Serial...
entry.content:
Hello!

The system requests serial numbers in purchase orders because the serial number profile currently includes the purchasing serialization procedure (POSL). In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, serialization behavior is fully controlled by the assigned serial number profile, and individual procedures can’t be removed once included. To capture serial numbers only at goods receipt, you need to use a serial number profile that excludes POSL and supports serialization only for goods movements.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can’t be deleted or removed individual serialization procedures from a serial number profile, can’t be changed the behavior inherited from the reference profile. Needs to be created a serial number profile without POSL.

In the following document you can find more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/960eb753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US&q=Serial+Number+Profile

 

Additional links:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/cannot-remove-the-serial-number-profile-in-change-material-app/qaq-p/13789717

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/ec28b753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US&q=Serial+Numbers+in+Purchasing

entry.title: Fullstack CAP Application with HANA Cloud (Decoupled Architecture) [Part-6]
entry.content:
Building and Deploying the Fiori App to HTML5 Repository

In the previous parts we:

✔ Built and tested the CAP backend locally (Part 1  & Part 2 )
✔ Set up SAP HANA Cloud on BTP (Part 3 )
✔ Deployed the CAP backend to Cloud Foundry (Part 4 )
✔ Configured the BTP Destination to point to the CAP service (Part 5 )

In this Part, we will:
- Generate the Fiori Elements app using the SAP Fiori Application Generator
- Add deployment configuration for Cloud Foundry and the HTML5 Repository
- Build and deploy the Fiori app as a standalone HTML5 application
- Access the fully working end-to-end application via SAP Build Work Zone

 

PREREQUISITES

Before proceeding, make sure the following are in place:

- VS Code with the SAP Fiori Tools Extension Pack installed / Business Application Studio (BAS)
- Cloud Foundry CLI logged in (cf login)
- The CAP backend deployed and running (from Part 4)
- The destination "destination-trial" configured in BTP (from Part 5)

> IMPORTANT — You also need the following active subscriptions in your BTP Subaccount before you can access the deployed Fiori app:
> - SAP HTML5 Application Repository Service
> - SAP Cloud Identity Services
> - SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition (for the Launchpad)

> Follow the step-by-step guide here  to enable these.

 

PART A — DEVELOPING THE FIORI UI APP

STEP 1 — OPEN THE SAP FIORI APPLICATION GENERATOR

In VS Code/BAS, press Ctrl+Shift+P and search for: SAP Fiori: Open Application Generator

The SAP Fiori Generator panel opens with a multi-step wizard on the left.

 

STEP 2 — SELECT THE TEMPLATE

On the Template Selection screen, choose: List Report Page

This template generates a two-page Fiori Elements app — a List Report for browsing records and an Object Page for viewing/editing individual records.
It uses the CDS Annotations we defined in Part 2 to render the UI automatically.

 Click "Next >".

 

STEP 3 — DATA SOURCE AND SERVICE SELECTION

Data Source:  Connect to an OData Service

 Click "Next >". The generator fetches the metadata from the live service.

STEP 4 — ENTITY SELECTION

Once the metadata is loaded, select:

Main Entity: Incidents
Navigation Entity: None
Generate Annotations: Yes
Table Type: Responsive

The generator will pick up the CDS Annotations we defined in Part 2 automatically since "Generate Annotations" is set to Yes.

Click "Next >".

STEP 5 — PROJECT ATTRIBUTES

Fill in the project details:

 Click "Finish". The generator scaffolds the complete Fiori Elements project in the specified folder. This creates the "incidentmanagement" app folder with webapp/, manifest.json, xs-app.json, and all config files.

STEP 6 — RUN AND TEST APP LOCALLY

npm run start-noflp

 

PART B — DEPLOYING THE FIORI APP TO HTML5 REPOSITORY

STEP 7 — ADD DEPLOYMENT CONFIGURATION

Navigate into the Fiori app folder and run:

npm run deploy-config

This command launches an interactive prompt to configure the deployment.


Answer the prompts as follows:

Target environment: Cloud Foundry
Destination name: destination-trial
App Router: Managed Application Router

Running this command generates/updates two key files:

mta.yaml → Multi-Target Application descriptor for CF deployment.
Defines three CF resources to be created:
- incidentmanagement-destination-service (destination, lite)→ includes a built-in "ui5" destination for SAPUI5 resources
- incidentmanagement-uaa (XSUAA, application plan)→ OAuth2 security for the app, configured via xs-security.json
- incidentmanagement-repo-host (html5-apps-repo, app-host)→ the HTML5 Application Repository bucket that stores the app

ui5-deploy.yaml → UI5 build configuration.
Packages the app into incidentmanagement.zip (excluding test/ and localService/ folders) and bundles xs-app.json into the archive for AppRouter routing rules.

 

STEP 8 — BUILD THE MTA ARCHIVE

Run the build command:

npm run build:mta

This command:
- Runs npm install and npm run build:cf to compile the UI5 application
- Packages the compiled app into incidentmanagement.zip
- Bundles everything into a deployable MTA archive: mta_archives/incidentmanagement_0.0.1.mtar

 

STEP 9 — DEPLOY TO CLOUD FOUNDRY

Deploy the Fiori app MTA archive to Cloud Foundry:

cf deploy mta_archives/incidentmanagement_0.0.1.mtar

During deployment, CF will automatically:
1. Create the XSUAA service instance (incidentmanagement-xsuaa-service)
2. Create the HTML5 App Repository host (incidentmanagement-html5-service)
3. Create the Destination service instance (incidentmanagement-destination-service)— including the built-in "ui5" destination for SAPUI5 CDN resources
4. Uploads the Fiori app zip to the HTML5 Repository
5. Register the app's destination content (html5 repo host + XSUAA token exchange)

STEP 10 — VERIFY AND ACCESS LIVE APPLICATION IN HTML5 REPO

In the BTP Cockpit, navigate to your Subaccount and click:

HTML5 Applications (in the left navigation)

You will see the deployed Fiori app listed under the Managed Application Router provided by SAP Build Work Zone:

 

Click on "incidentmanagement" in the HTML5 Applications page to open the app in SAP Build Work Zone Launchpad.

The Fiori Elements List Report opens in the browser — now running fully in the cloud, reading live data from SAP HANA Cloud via the CAP service:

 
SERIES SUMMARY:

Over 6 parts, we built and deployed a complete, production-grade Incident Management application on SAP BTP using modern SAP technologies:

Part 1  → CAP Node.js project with domain model, OData services,CSV seed data — running locally on SQLite

Part 2  → CDS Annotations for the Fiori Elements UI:field labels, list/object page layout, value helps

Part 3  → SAP HANA Cloud instance (SHC_DB) on BTP — entitlements,roles, instance creation, CF environment mapping

Part 4  → CAP backend deployed to Cloud Foundry, HDI containercreated, schema deployed, hybrid mode with cds bind

Part 5  → BTP Destination (destination-trial) configured to bridge the Fiori app to the CAP service URL

Part 6 (This Part) → Fiori Elements app generated, configured for HTML5 Repo, deployed to Cloud Foundry — live end-to-end on BTP

The result is a fully decoupled architecture where the CAP backend and the Fiori frontend are deployed and scaled independently a pattern recommended by SAP for modern BTP applications.

I hope this series helped you understand how CAP, HANA Cloud, Fiori Elements, and BTP services come together to build a real application.

 In the coming parts, we will get to know about configuring the App Router, understanding xs-security.json, adding validations, implementing custom actions, securing our app with XSUAA, and even more. Stay tuned!

entry.title: How SAP S/4HANA supports operational complexity in the Oil & Gas industry
entry.content:
Introduction

When I think of the most difficult fields to work with, the first one to come to my mind has to be, inevitably, Oil and Gas. I think everyone can agree with me when I say that Oil and Gas companies are extremely hard to operate, as they do so in environments where complexity is the standard: many different parts of the business depend on each other simultaneously. Daily operations depend on large networks of assets, field activities, supply chains, maintenance cycles, compliance requirements, and financial controls that all need to work together in real time.

From the outside, it is easy to assume that with these types of companies the biggest issue would be related to the size of the operations, since they tend to have thousands of employees, global operations and massive amounts of data. Nonetheless, the real challenge is making all departments, systems, and processes work together without difficulties. In other words, it is a problem of coordination, not of size.

Over the years, the system these companies manage starts changing little by little, which leads to different teams working differently instead of following one single and consistent process, and they also begin to work with varied versions of operational data. We are talking about an environment that, as time passes by, becomes harder and harder to manage.

This is where SAP S/4HANA becomes important, not because it eliminates complexity, but because it helps organizations operate through it with greater visibility and control.

When operational complexity starts creating friction

Oil and Gas companies are, in some aspects, the same as any other type of business. One of these similarities they share is that problems don’t suddenly appear one night and explode the following day. As the company evolves, small changes, which at the time may seem harmless, accumulate. Maybe a new software is added or a different way of working among employees is incorporated. Teams become more independent as they gain confidence and news acquisitions, with their respected technology and processes, are added. All these things that are necessary in the short-term cause work to become fragmented and an environment where even the most simple activities, like generating reports or tracking inventory, is hard to manage.

We have seen cases where different departments within the same company are technically using operational data, but they are not using it in a synchronized way. This is one of the main reasons the company becomes slower and less efficient. I will mention, as examples, the maintenance team, the procurement team, and the finance department. In these cases, everyone was looking at operations from different perspectives and with different update speeds. Although everyone had the information, they had it distributed across multiple systems and processes that did not communicate consistently with each other. As a result, operational planning became slower, manual intervention was required more often, and decision-making became slower as the data couldn’t be used efficiently.  Not to mention the fact that, in Oil and Gas companies, this is particularly critical because a minimal maintenance delay can affect production schedules and have financial and operational consequences simultaneously.

SAP S/4HANA helps reduce this fragmentation by acting as a shared operational environment for the whole company. Instead of every department using separate systems and isolated workflows, they all use the same operational framework. SAP integration allows areas that depend heavily on each other to exist inside one connected environment with information being consistently shared across the whole organization.

I came across a situation like this some years ago, when we worked with a global company that supports energy and field operations around the world. This company had a very large amount of operational purchasing activity, but many of the SAP processes behind it were way too manual compared to the size of the business. When the company contacted us, they had already went through problems during maintenance shutdowns because a lot of sites were sending urgent material requests at the same time through SAP.

To help with this, we automated part of the SAP purchasing and approval process so urgent requests could move faster and teams didn’t have to spend so much time manually checking and coordinating everything. This helped them process materials faster during maintenance periods and reduced a lot of repetitive admin work between operations and procurement teams.

And that is my point: SAP S/4HANA, at least in the Oil and Gas environment, is the easiest and safest way of connecting processes in real time. One of the biggest operational pressures in Oil and Gas environments comes from the fact that many activities cannot simply “wait until tomorrow.” Maintenance schedules, field operations, procurement cycles, and inventory availability are often directly connected to production continuity. Even the smallest delay can affect the whole system in these kinds of fields.

One thing is clear: operational efficiency is not only related to production itself. In fact, if production goes well it’s because there is something else functioning behind it, and that has to do with how quickly companies are able to react when conditions change. This is one of the reasons why SAP S/4HANA has become increasingly important in asset-intensive industries. It allows organizations to build more connected operational environments where information can move faster between teams and operational areas. In industries where downtime, delays, or lack of visibility can quickly translate into operational and financial consequences, having more standardized and integrated processes becomes less of a technological improvement and more of an operational necessity.

Conclusion

In Oil & Gas, circumstances almost never slow down enough to permit a full reset. Teams have to keep the wheels turning as production can’t stop, maintenance issues pop up, materials need to be in the right place, and decisions have to be made fast, all under constant stress. Minor flaws in how companies do things can snowball into much bigger problems if no one catches them early.

That’s where SAP S/4HANA really shows its value. Sure, it covers all the basics, but it goes further: it helps companies build smoother, more connected operations. Processes speed up, data gets more reliable, and teams don’t have to waste so much time tangled in paperwork or chasing down info. In a fast-paced world like Oil & Gas, being able to respond quickly and stay consistent isn’t just nice to have, it’s essential.

entry.title: Mobile Services registered user custom fields / custom properties
entry.content:
In SAP Mobile Services, in Cloud Foundry -> under a Native Application, in User Registration -> if you click on any registration, there are 4 custom fields: custom1 to custom4 .

we would like to know how to populate those fields using the BTP SDK for iOS and also how to handle push notifications using those fields.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Mobile Services registered user custom fields / custo...
entry.content:
- Regarding the Custom Properties:

---

You are able to set those using the storage runtime API with the <registrationId> value you get from <userInfo>:


1. Get registration Id from userInfo firstly (https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/user-information/android/user-information.html#registration-id)

2. Patch Storage Service By Device Name (https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/storage/api/runtime.html#request-for-patch-storage-service-by-device-name) using the Registration Id as <deviceId> in request path, and the property path is "/mobileservices/settingsExchange/device".

For example:

[

PATCH <Server API>/mobileservices/application/<appName>/Storage/v1/runtime/application/<appName>/device/<deviceId>/mobileservices/settingsExchange/device
Body: {"customCustom1":"1","customCustom2":"2","customCustom3":"3","customCustom4":"4"}

]

---

- Regarding the Push service:

---

Please notice that the Push Service cannot use those Custom fields to select the recipients... instead, it supports different methods for the push notification producer to select the recipients, see below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/push/api/sending-push.html#defining-the-target-instances

Push to DeviceThe notification target is selected by username and device ID. The device ID is generated during device push registration.Push to UsersThe notification is delivered to all push registered devices of the users. The users is specified by user name or Global User ID.Bulk PushBulk Push allows to send multiple variations of a notification to multiple users in one request.Push to ApplicationThe notification is delivered to all push registered devices of the app.Push to TopicMobile apps can subscribe to a list of interests / topics. The provider can publish a notification to all or list of users that are subscribed to this topic.

Based on your description, a suitable method would be the "Push to Topic" (https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/push/api/sending-push.html#push-to-topic), where your devices can subscribe to a topic and the push producer will be able to send notifications to that topic. ¹ ²

¹ Notice that the BTP SDK for Android and iOS has methods to manage these subscriptions on device side as well.

² Authentication attributes can be retrieved by the device via User-Information service: https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/user-information/api/client.html

---

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: INSERT errors out
entry.content:
Hi, ALL,

[code]

queries.push_back( L"IF NOT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sysobjects WHERE name = 'abcatedt' AND type = 'U') EXECUTE(\"CREATE TABLE abcatedt(abe_name char(30) NOT NULL, abe_edit char(254), abe_type smallint, abe_cntr integer, abe_seqn smallint NOT NULL, abe_flag integer, abe_work char(32), PRIMARY KEY( abe_name, abe_seqn ))\")" );

queries.push_back( L"IF NOT EXISTS(SELECT o.name, i.name FROM sysobjects o, sysindexes i WHERE o.id = i.id AND o.name = 'abcatedt' AND i.name = 'abcate_x') EXECUTE(\"CREATE UNIQUE INDEX abcate_x ON abcatedt(abe_name ASC, abe_seqn ASC) WITH IGNORE_DUP_KEY\")");

[[/code]

After running those I tried to do this:

 

[code]

2084 for( std::vector<std::wstring>::iterator it = queries.begin(); it < queries.end(); ++it )
(gdb)
2086 qry.reset( new SQLWCHAR[(*it).length() + 2] );
(gdb)
2087 memset( qry.get(), '\0', (*it).length() + 2 );
(gdb)
2088 uc_to_str_cpy( qry.get(), (*it) );
(gdb)
2089 ret = SQLExecDirect( m_hstmt, qry.get(), SQL_NTS );
(gdb)
2090 if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO )
(gdb)
2092 GetErrorMessage( errorMsg, STMT_ERROR );
(gdb)
2093 ret = SQLEndTran( SQL_HANDLE_DBC, m_hdbc, SQL_ROLLBACK );
(gdb) p (*it)
$1 = (std::__cxx11::basic_string<wchar_t, std::char_traits<wchar_t>, std::allocator<wchar_t> > &) @0x6210005e12a0: {
static npos = 18446744073709551615,
_M_dataplus = {<std::allocator<wchar_t>> = {<__gnu_cxx::new_allocator<wchar_t>> = {<No data fields>}, <No data fields>},
_M_p = 0x6130000dc840 L"INSERT INTO abcatedt VALUES( 'BenefitsCheckBox', NULL, 85, 4, 1, 536870916, NULL );"}, _M_string_length = 83, {
_M_local_buf = L"S\000\xbebebebe\xbebebebe", _M_allocated_capacity = 83}}
(gdb) p errorMsg.at(0)
$2 = (__gnu_cxx::__alloc_traits<std::allocator<std::__cxx11::basic_string<wchar_t, std::char_traits<wchar_t>, std::allocator<wchar_t> > >, std::__cxx11::basic_string<wchar_t, std::char_traits<wchar_t>, std::allocator<wchar_t> > >::value_type &) @0x606000233840: {
static npos = 18446744073709551615,
_M_dataplus = {<std::allocator<wchar_t>> = {<__gnu_cxx::new_allocator<wchar_t>> = {<No data fields>}, <No data fields>},
_M_p = 0x615000194b80 L"[SAP][ASE ODBC Driver][Adaptive Server Enterprise]The column abe_edit in table abcatedt does not allow null values.\n"}, _M_string_length = 116, {_M_local_buf = L"t\000\xbebebebe\xbebebebe", _M_allocated_capacity = 116}}
(gdb)

[/code]

 

I don't understand the error.

The field abe_edit should allow NULLs - it does not have "NOT NULL" clause.

 

Could someone please sched some lights?

 

I have SAP ASE 16..1 on Linux.

The connection is done thru the ODBC driver with unixODBC Driver Manager.

 

Thank you for any pointers...

P.S.: For some reason SAP ASE tag is not available to me. If someone can fix it and place the proper tag here it would be great.

P.P.S.: I tried to add:

 

[code]

if( !result )
{
memset( qry.get(), '\0', 200 );
uc_to_str_cpy( qry.get(), L"sp_dboption tempdb, 'allow nulls by default', 'true'" );
ret = SQLExecDirect( m_hstmt, qry.get(), SQL_NTS );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO )
{
GetErrorMessage( errorMsg, STMT_ERROR );
result = 1;
}
}

entry.title: Re: SAC Planning Master Data Mapping for Cost Category (M...
entry.content:
Thank you for the clarification. Just to make sure I understand correctly, my understanding is as follows:

  • We keep the mapping table within the same SAC model (rather than in a separate model), and store it in a separate “audit trail” such as a dedicated Version.
  • When uploading the mapping table, we hardcode the Measure value to 1.
  • Then, using a Data Action, we multiply the unmapped transaction data (where the relevant dimensions are “#”) by the mapping-table data (value = 1), so that the transaction amounts are written only to the mapped target intersections.
  • This approach assumes there is a unique 1:1 mapping.

Could you please confirm whether this understanding is correct?

entry.title: Re: Considerations when implementing IAS for SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Since going live with IAS for Onboarding, we’ve seen a significant increase in candidate support requirements that were previously manageable by Recruitment (e.g. password resets and restarting onboarding).

We’re encountering two main issues:

1. Loss of candidate access post–start date

If onboarding is not completed before the candidate’s start date, our downstream Active Directory (via third-party writeback) updates the primary email address to the business email.

As this process cannot be amended or revoked on our side, it creates the following problem:

  • Candidates can no longer log in using their personal email address
  • This requires manual intervention to:
    • Update the email address in IAS
    • Reset/reconfigure passwords
    • Contact the candidate directly

Given how frequently candidates don’t complete onboarding prior to their start date, this has effectively become a daily operational task for our team.

2. Failed Onboarding → Employee conversions

Since go-live, we’ve also experienced intermittent failures in the conversion process, even where onboarding has been fully completed. There’s no clear pattern — it appears to be random.

Symptoms include:

  • “Onboarding New Recruit” label persisting in People Profile
  • Data Inspector showing the User Account is still 'e' in valid 
  • Downstream integrations not triggering until conversion completes

Our implementation partner provided a workaround:

  • Manage Data → Process → Update Process and Task Status to Open → Add new update in Job UI

However:

  • This workaround is inconsistent
  • If unsuccessful, they must run backend scripts

The explanation provided by SAP points to the target date matching the hire date, but this is also true for successfully converted users, so it doesn’t fully explain the issue.

Question to the community

Has anyone else experienced:

  • Similar IAS/email identity challenges impacting candidate access?
  • Random ONB → EC conversion failures despite completed onboarding?

@NathanWilkinson  – I’d also be interested to understand how your customers are handling Onboarding team access within IAS.

Given the volume of affected users, requiring a specialist team with elevated (admin-level) access to manually resolve these issues introduces both:

  • Operational inefficiency, and
  • Potential risk exposure

Appreciate any insights or comparable experiences.

entry.title: Re: Vendor invoice reverse MR8M
entry.content:
Hello @leah_huang71 ,

The error occurs because your freight invoice was posted in EUR while the PO is in USD, causing SAP to create internal exchange rate/valuation amounts. During MR8M reversal, SAP detects these “reverse values” are still active and blocks reversal with M8283.

-Analyze exchange rate difference postings and invoice valuation entries first
-Ensure your BAPI populates currency/exchange-rate fields correctly
-Reset/reverse valuation-related entries before running MR8M again.

 

Issue / SituationExplanation, Root Cause & SAP Notes
Error M8283 during MR8M reversalThis error usually occurs when the invoice contains foreign currency valuation / exchange rate differences that have not been reset before reversal.
Your scenario✔ PO currency = USD ✔ Freight invoice currency = EUR ✔ Invoice posted via BAPI in EUR ✔ Open item still uncleared
Root causeSince invoice currency differs from PO currency, SAP creates currency translation values (exchange rate differences). MR8M cannot reverse the document while the “reverse value” / valuation status is still active.
Why this happens especially with BAPISome BAPI postings do not fully synchronize the valuation/reversal indicators the same way as MIRO standard processing, especially in multi-currency freight scenarios.
Most common technical causeExchange rate difference entries exist in GR/IR or invoice valuation, and SAP expects them to be reset before reversal.
Check if document has valuationVerify whether document has: ✔ Exchange rate differences ✔ Subsequent debit/credit relation ✔ LIV valuation entries
Important pointEven if open item is not cleared, MR8M may still block reversal because valuation amounts are active internally.
Recommended first checkRun MRBR / MIR4 and analyze invoice history + accounting document for FX difference postings.
Recommended solutionReverse/reset the related valuation or exchange rate difference first, then perform MR8M.
Alternative workaroundIf posted via custom BAPI logic, validate whether BAPI correctly populated: ✔ Currency fields ✔ Exchange rate fields ✔ Header/item currency consistency
Tables to analyzeCheck: ✔ RBKP ✔ RSEG ✔ BSEG ✔ BKPF ✔ EKBE

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: PDC
entry.content:
Hello @Yuvraj_Singh_Rawat ,

The best practice is to treat the Third Party as a separate financing creditor/liability. After Vendor X is paid by the Third Party, transfer the liability from Vendor X to the Third Party, maintain the repayment due date separately, and post interest as an additional adjustment entry at maturity instead of modifying the original invoice amount directly.

 

Requirement / ScenarioRecommended SAP Standard Process & Best Practice
Vendor invoice raised by Vendor XPost normal vendor invoice against Vendor X using MIRO/FB60.
Third Party pays Vendor X on behalf of companyTreat the Third Party as a financing partner / loan creditor rather than a normal vendor payment process.
Liability shifts from Vendor X to Third PartyAfter Third Party settles Vendor X, clear Vendor X liability and create a new payable toward the Third Party.
Recommended accounting entry✔ Dr Vendor X Payable ✔ Cr Third Party Liability Account
90-day repayment requirementMaintain payment terms or due date on the Third Party payable item (FB60/FB01/F-02).
Interest added at maturityBest practice is to post interest separately at maturity rather than modifying original principal document.
Requirement to change due date✔ Standard SAP supports changing baseline date/payment terms using: FBL1N / FB02 (depending on field status and open item status).
Requirement to change final amount including interest❌ Standard SAP does not recommend changing posted invoice amount directly. ✔ Preferred approach: post separate interest adjustment document.
Best practice for financing scenariosUse: ✔ Vendor invoice + separate financing liability ✔ Or SAP Treasury / FSCM if process is frequent and complex
If process is recurringConsider using: ✔ Loan Management ✔ Treasury Transaction Manager ✔ Supplier Financing / Supply Chain Finance solution
Audit & compliance recommendationAvoid changing posted principal amounts directly; use adjustment/interest postings for transparency and auditability.


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Some Scenario's SAP_WFRT automaticaly execute the Pay...
entry.content:
Hello @md_wasim_akhtar36 ,

The automatic payment execution is not caused directly by F110 itself, but most likely by a workflow post-processing/background step executed by SAP_WFRT/WF-BATCH after approval. Since these steps run in background, normal debugging often does not trigger. You should analyze the workflow instance/event flow (SWI1/SWEL/SWDD) and remove or bypass the automatic execution step so that approval only releases the proposal and Treasury can manually execute the payment run later.

 

Issue / ObservationExplanation, Root Cause & Recommendation
Payment run sometimes executes automatically after approvalThis behavior usually occurs because the workflow template or post-processing logic contains an automatic scheduling/execution step after approval.
Why it happens only sometimesDifferent payment runs may follow different workflow branches depending on: ✔ Payment method ✔ Amount thresholds ✔ Workflow conditions ✔ Background job timing
SAP_WFRT / WF-BATCH executing paymentThis confirms the payment run is being triggered by a background workflow step, not manually by user.
Your expected process✔ Proposal approval only ✔ No automatic payment execution ✔ Treasury manually executes payment later
Most likely root causeThe workflow post-processing logic or standard workflow step is configured to execute F110 payment run automatically after approval.
Why your BAdI debugging did not triggerFI_PAYMENT_PROPOSAL_WF may not be the actual execution point for the automatic payment scheduling. In many cases the execution is triggered via: ✔ Background task ✔ Workflow method ✔ Event linkage ✔ Batch job
Why session/external breakpoints failWorkflow steps executed by SAP_WFRT / WF-BATCH run in background work processes, so normal breakpoints often do not trigger.
Correct debugging approachUse: ✔ Update debugging ✔ Workflow trace (SWI1 / SWEL / SWELS) ✔ Background user debugging (JDBG/SM37)
Recommended analysisCheck workflow template for: ✔ Automatic post-processing step ✔ Method calling payment execution ✔ Event linkage after approval
Important transactions✔ SWI1 – Workflow instances ✔ SWEL – Workflow events ✔ SM37 – Background jobs ✔ SWDD – Workflow definition
Recommended fixRemove or bypass the workflow step that automatically schedules payment execution after approval. Approval should only release proposal status.
Alternative safeguardAdd validation logic preventing payment execution when initiated by SAP_WFRT/WF-BATCH.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: I've already did all required configuration but not t...
entry.content:
Hello @hemja ,

If parked document workflow is not triggering, the issue is usually one of these: inactive event linkage (SWETYPV), workflow template not activated, incorrect start conditions, or no event being raised at all. The fastest way to isolate the issue is to activate event trace (SWELS) and check SWEL after parking the document. That immediately tells you whether the problem is event generation, workflow linkage, or agent determination.

 

Check AreaWhat to Verify
Workflow event linkageVerify event linkage is active in SWETYPV. Most common issue is event linkage not activated.
Workflow template activeEnsure workflow template is released/active in PFTC or SWDD.
Event actually triggeredCheck event log in SWEL. If no event appears after parking document, issue is before workflow start.
Receiver determinationVerify agent determination/rules are working correctly. Missing approver can prevent workflow start.
Parked document typeEnsure your workflow is configured for the same parked document type/company code you are testing.
Workflow activationMake sure workflow runtime/event queue is active using SWU3.
Event queue stuckCheck if events are stuck in queue via SM58 or SWF_XI_ADM_BPE.
Start conditionsVerify workflow start conditions are not filtering out your document (amount, company code, doc type, etc.).
User authorizationEnsure workflow user/initiator has authorization to trigger workflow events.
Standard object/eventFor parked FI documents, standard object/event is usually related to FI document parking events — verify correct BUS object and event are used.
Workflow log analysisUse SWI1 to check whether workflow instance was created and failed immediately.


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: New GL Migration
entry.content:
You do not use the S/4 Migration Cockpit for this activity. The correct standard SAP tool is FAGL_MIG_COCKPIT for migrating from Classic GL to New GL in ECC before S/4HANA conversion. SAP migration services are optional, but due to the complexity (document splitting, ledgers, reconciliation, open items), many customers use experienced SAP Finance migration consultants or partners.

 

Question / TopicExplanation & Best Practice
Can S/4HANA conversion be done without New GL?❌ No. S/4HANA requires migration to New GL / Universal Journal compatibility first. Classic GL is not supported in S/4HANA.
Your scenarioECC system with 10 years production data and Classic GL (no New GL active).
Recommended approachPerform Classic GL → New GL migration first, then proceed with S/4HANA conversion.
Do you need SAP Migration Service?❌ Not mandatory. Many customers perform migration themselves or with implementation partners. SAP service is optional for complex/high-risk projects.
Is this done via Migration Cockpit?❌ No. This is NOT done using S/4 Migration Cockpit (LTMC / LTMOM).
Correct migration toolUse the SAP New GL Migration Cockpit.
Migration transaction code✔ FAGL_MIG_COCKPIT
Purpose of FAGL_MIG_COCKPITUsed to migrate: ✔ Classic GL balances ✔ Open items ✔ Documents ✔ Profit center updates into New GL structures
How to activate migration cockpitActivate required business functions and install New GL migration components via SAP Notes/customizing. Usually available once New GL migration preparation is configured.
Key migration phases1️⃣ Preparation 2️⃣ Consistency checks 3️⃣ Activate New GL 4️⃣ Document split/profit center migration (if needed) 5️⃣ Migration run 6️⃣ Reconciliation
Biggest challengeNot data volume, but: ✔ Open item consistency ✔ Profit center balancing ✔ Document splitting ✔ Parallel ledgers
Downtime impactMigration usually requires controlled cutover and downtime planning.
Recommended preparationRun: ✔ Readiness checks ✔ Ledger analysis ✔ Reconciliation reports before starting migration
S/4HANA conversion after migrationOnce New GL migration is stable and reconciled, proceed with SUM/DMO conversion to S/4HANA.


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Question Regarding Historical Reporting Behavior After Profit Center Hierarchy Change
entry.content:
Dear experts,

We have a profit center that originally belonged to Profit Center Group 1, and expense postings had already been recorded under this assignment.

Due to management requirements, the profit center hierarchy assignment was later changed, and the profit center is now assigned to Profit Center Group 2.

However, we found that the historical expense postings are also being reported under Profit Center Group 2 after the hierarchy change.

Is there any standard method or recommended approach to achieve the following behavior?

  • Expenses posted before the hierarchy change are reported under Profit Center Group 1
  • Expenses posted after the hierarchy change are reported under Profit Center Group 2

Could you please advise whether this is possible in SAP standard functionality, or if any workaround/enhancement would be required?

Please suggest！
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

entry.title: How to Post Movement Types 313 and 315 Using API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Introduction

Recently I worked on posting stock transfer movements using the standard OData service API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV in SAP S/4HANA. The requirement was to perform transfer posting between storage locations using movement type 313 and 315 through API testing in SAP Gateway Client (/n/IWFND/GW_CLIENT)

During this process, I faced multiple technical and business validation error such as HTT 405, Bad request (400), XML parse issues, and stock in Transfer errors. This blog explains the troubleshooting steps and the final successful payload structure.

Service Used:

/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV/A_MaterialDocumentHeader

HTTP Method: POST

Goods Movement Code: 04 (Transfer Posting)

Common Errors Faced and Resolutions:

1. HTT 405 - Method Not Allowed

Cause: POST method executed on wrong URI or service root

Solution: Use the correct entity set:

/A_MaterialDocumentHeader

instead of service root or metadata URL

2. HTTP 400 - System Query Options Error

Cause: Using: ?$format=json

with POST request

Solution: Remove query options from POST URL

3. XML Parse Error

Cause: Incorrect JSON structure for deep insert

Wrong: "to_MaterialDocumentItem": [

Correct: "to_MaterialDocumentItem": {

                  "results": [

Key Learning:

Using standard API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV fro material movement posting is powerful but requirs careful attention to:

  1. Correct entity set
  2. proper JSON structure
  3. Open Posting Periods
  4. Correct business sequence (313 -> 315)

Once these are handled, the API works smoothly and supports seamless automation for stock transfer postings.

Test Case Scenario

Stock Overview before posting 313

(Note: stock available in unrestricted use for material 14000 under batch C1)

Navigate to transaction /n/IWFND/GW_CLIENT and click on service API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV --> Click on SAP Gateway Client

 

Click on Entity Set

Select A_MaterialDocumentHeader and continue

Pass HTTP Request in JSON format to post 313 movement

(Note: make sure you selected POST method, and correct URL)

Movement type 313 is used to move stock into transfer stock.

Sample JSON Payload

{

  "PostingDate": "2026-01-15T00:00:00",

  "GoodsMovementCode": "04",

  "MaterialDocumentHeaderText": "RM01 to WH01",

  "to_MaterialDocumentItem": {

    "results": [

      {

        "Material": "14000",

        "Plant": "2100",

        "StorageLocation": "WH01",

        "GoodsMovementType": "313",

        "QuantityInEntryUnit": "1",

        "EntryUnit": "PC",

        "IssuingOrReceivingPlant": "2100",

        "IssuingOrReceivingStorageLoc": "RM01",

        "Batch": "C1"

      }

    ]

  }

}

Once Executed, HTTP Response displayed with response code 200 (you can find the material document)

Check Stock, it must update in stock in transfer in destination location

Display 313 material document in MIGO transaction

Pass HTTP Request in JSON format to post 315 movement

(Note: make sure you selected POST method, and correct URL)

Movement type 315 is used to clear stock in transfer and complete the movement.

Sample JSON Payload

{

  "PostingDate": "2026-01-15T00:00:00",

  "GoodsMovementCode": "04",

  "MaterialDocumentHeaderText": "Place in RM01",

  "to_MaterialDocumentItem": {

    "results": [

      {

        "Material": "14000",

        "Plant": "2100",

        "StorageLocation": "WH01",

        "GoodsMovementType": "315",

        "QuantityInEntryUnit": "1",

        "EntryUnit": "PC",

        "IssuingOrReceivingPlant": "2100",

        "IssuingOrReceivingStorageLoc": "WH01",

        "Batch": "C1"

      }

    ]

  }

}

Once Executed, HTTP Response displayed with response code 200 (you can find the material document)

Stock updated in Unrestricted Use in destination location under same batch C1

Display 315 Material Document in MIGO transaction

READ posted 313 or 315 document using GET method

(Note: Pass filter URI as mention in below image and mention material document number, and make sure you selected GET method)

Once Executed, HTTP Response displayed with response code 200 (you can display the material document details)

 

If you found this guide helpful, don't miss any other SAP MM WM blogs covering real-world automation, configurations tips, and best practices for procurement, inventory management, and warehouse management.

Click to view other SAP MM WM Blogs - SAP Community

 

Regards,

Mannem Leelamohan

entry.title: Automate User Lifecycle Management [SAP IAS]: Block or Delete with Confidence
entry.content:
Common Use case across IT Landscape :
Users who have not logged into SAP applications for the last 90 days to be automatically set to Block status.  IT administrators and application owners to easily identify inactive or unused accounts, improving security visibility and control.
 
Introducing this feature for SAP IAS - Tenant administrator can now configure specific user types to be blocked and deleted, or only blocked, or only deleted after a certain period of inactivity.
 
The users for which blocking can be enabled are of the type:
  • Public
  • Customers
  • Partners
  • Employees
You can enable the chosen user type or types to be only blocked, or only deleted, or both blocked and deleted after a certain period of inactivity.
The period spans from 14 up to 2000 days.

To enable blocking and or deletion, you must choose at least one user type and set at least one period.

After a user has exceeded the set period of inactivity, they will be blocked and their status will be updated on the next sign-in attempt.

You can also configure when the deletion script starts.

Steps to enable 

  1. Sign in to the administration console for SAP Cloud Identity Services.
  2. Under Applications and Resources, choose the Tenant Settings tile.

    At the top of the page, you can view the administrative and license relevant information of the tenant.

  3. Choose the Blocking and Deletion list item.
  4. Choose Edit.
  5. Select a user type.
  6. Enable the options under Inactivity Periods. By default the options are disabled. At least one of the status fields Block after or Delete after must be enabled.
How to find the Last Logon Date of the User to start the Inactivity Blocking User.

User Persona (Examples)

By configuring a 90-day inactivity rule, organizations can automatically block users who have not accessed SAP applications, allowing IT administrators and application owners to quickly identify dormant accounts and strengthen overall security governance.
Mixed User Persona for Block & Deletion
 

By tailoring inactivity rules for different user types, organizations can strike the perfect balance between security, compliance, and user experience—turning identity lifecycle management into a strategic advantage

 

Reference : https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/block-or-delete-users-due-to-inactivity?version=Cloud

entry.title: Re: Confused in taking the courses.
entry.content:
Right now your question leaves some gaps. For example, you have not mentioned your educational background, how many years of IT/domain experience you have, or which areas of IT you are strongest in. These details matter because SAP has multiple tracks (technical, functional, and hybrid), and the best starting point depends on your profile.

Here is a practical way forward:

1) Clarify your background
Note your degree, IT specialization (networking, databases, support, etc.), and years of experience.

2) Pick a starting module
If you are more technical → look at SAP Basis/Administration or SAP ABAP (development). If you want functional/business process work → explore SAP MM (Materials Management), SAP SD (Sales & Distribution), or SAP FI (Finance).

3) Courses
Begin with SAP Learning Hub (official) or openSAP free courses to get structured knowledge.

4) Certifications
Once you identify your track, aim for an entry-level certification (e.g., SAP Certified Application Associate in your chosen module).

Try to get access to a training system or sandbox to practice transactions and scenarios.

My advice would be first decide whether you want to go technical (system/development) or functional (business processes), then pick one module and build depth. That focus will make your transition smoother.

entry.title: Re: How To unprotect Excel sheet without password?
entry.content:
If you forgot the password of your Excel sheet, then there are several ways available to unprotect the file without a password. Many users first try manual techniques because they are free and easily available online. Methods like VBA code and changing the Excel file extension can sometimes help remove normal worksheet or workbook protection from Excel files. These approaches are mainly useful when the file only has editing restrictions and not strong encryption.

Some commonly used manual methods include:

  • Using VBA code to bypass worksheet protection
  • Changing XLSX file extension to ZIP and editing internal XML files
  • Saving the workbook in another format and reopening it
  • Trying previously used or partially remembered passwords
  • Using Microsoft-recommended recovery options

Although these methods may work in some situations, they also have many limitations. VBA scripts usually fail in newer Excel versions, and changing the file format manually can be confusing for non-technical users. In some cases, incorrect editing may even corrupt the Excel workbook. Most importantly, these tricks cannot remove “Password to Open” security because modern Excel files use strong AES encryption technology.

If your Excel file is encrypted with a complex password, then manual methods are usually not enough. In such cases, using a professional solution becomes necessary. SysTools Excel Password Recovery Tool  is one of the most reliable solutions to recover lost or forgotten Excel passwords legally and securely. The software supports both old and new Excel file formats, including XLS and XLSX.

Key highlights of the tool:

  • Recover lost or forgotten Excel passwords smoothly
  • Supports workbook and worksheet password recovery
  • Can recover “Password to Open” protected files
  • Works with AES encrypted Excel files
  • Supports unknown and partially known passwords
  • Maintains complete data integrity
  • Compatible with multiple Microsoft Excel versions
  • No advanced technical knowledge required
  • Fast and reliable password recovery process

Therefore, if basic manual methods fail to unprotect your Excel sheet, then using a trusted professional solution is the safest option to recover the password without risking your important data.

entry.title: How to handle payment workflow in Public Cloud without J78 activation?
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

How to handle payment workflow in Public Cloud without J78 activation?

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to handle payment workflow in Public Cloud withou...
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you for raising this question in SAP Community.

To enable approval workflow for payment runs in the Manage Automatic Payments app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there are two prerequisites that must be in place:

  • Activation of the Scope Advanced Cash Operations (J78) is required.
  • In the configuration activity Define Basic Settings (SSCUI ID 101025) cash scope must be selected as Full Scope.

At this time, there is no way to enable this functionality without the activation of the scope item J78. If approval workflow for payment runs is needed in your landscape, please consider this option.

For more details regarding payment approval workflow, see KBA 3731235 - Enabling approval workflow for payment runs in Manage Automatic Payments app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me.

Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: Re: Question Regarding Historical Reporting Behavior Afte...
entry.content:
Workarounds / Recommended approaches

1) Time-dependent reporting
Use custom reporting logic (e.g., CDS views or analytical queries) that captures the hierarchy assignment valid at the time of posting. This requires storing snapshots of hierarchy assignments externally or via custom fields.

2) Alternative structures
Instead of reassigning the profit center, create a new profit center under Group 2 for future postings. This way, historical postings remain under Group 1, and new postings naturally fall under Group 2.

3) Custom enhancement
Build a reporting enhancement (via SAP BTP or in-app extensibility) to simulate “time-dependent hierarchy” by combining posting date with hierarchy change date. This is not standard, but it is the only way to achieve the split view you described.

Having said that, the recommendation is if management requires clear separation of “before vs after” reporting, the best practice is to create a new profit center instead of reassigning the existing one. That preserves historical reporting integrity. If the reassignment has already been done, you’ll need a custom reporting solution to reconstruct the old hierarchy view.

entry.title: Re: Change log report for reservation
entry.content:
Some workarounds are

1) Change Document Objects (Custom Development)
Create a custom change document object for reservations. Use BAdIs or user exits (e.g., MB_CHANGE_BADI) to trigger logging whenever a reservation is changed. Store logs in a Z‑table with fields like reservation number, item, old value, new value, user, timestamp.

2) Enhance MB22/MB25 Reporting
Add a custom tab or ALV report that reads from your Z‑change log table. This can mimic the “Environment” tab behavior from ME22N.

3) Workflow/Notification Approach
Trigger workflow events on reservation changes. Capture who changed what and send notifications or store audit records.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Deleting Inbound Delivery (LA) – Error VL111
entry.content:
Why you can’t delete is because

  • The inbound delivery for 33 qty is linked to the PO and confirmations.  
  • The GM status is updated once any GR is posted against the PO line item, even if it’s for a different delivery.
  • Because stock from the earlier GR (55 qty) has already been consumed, reversal isn’t possible, so the system prevents deletion of the later delivery to maintain consistency.


Options to resolve

1) Set delivery quantity to zero
Instead of deleting, reduce the delivery item quantity to zero in VL32N. This effectively cancels the delivery without breaking the GM status rules.

2) Use reversal/cancellation if stock is available
If stock from the earlier GR were still available, you could reverse the GR (MBST/MIGO), then cancel the delivery. Since stock is consumed, this path is blocked.

3) Close the delivery via completion
Mark the delivery item as “completed” so it no longer appears as open. This is a standard way to handle unwanted deliveries when deletion isn’t possible.

4) Business process workaround
Leave the 33‑qty delivery in the system but ensure no GR is posted against it. Use reporting filters to exclude it from open delivery lists.

So the recommendation in your case is, the safest approach to change the delivery item quantity to zero in VL32N. This cancels the delivery without violating SAP’s GM status rules. Deletion is not possible once GR has been posted and stock consumed

Also, please check SAP Note 3628531

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.3: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.3 is now available.

To access the final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer. In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.3 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access it.

For you as a customer, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool. The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.3 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

You can use the commenting feature for What’s New. With this feature, authors can evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: Re: Aligning 3 SAP systems (CUSTOM, TEST, PRD) with the s...
entry.content:
Hi @bryan_mendoza71 !

Regarding SAP roles, profiles, security changes - the best practise is clear: NEVER start creating/changing directly in PRD! Do not generate chaos, use DEV/CUSTOM system. Once finished, move it by transport request to TST, once confirmed working by positive + negative tests, let it go into PRD.

Any exception is the road to hell. Believe me, I've been there...

Regarding SAP Systemcopy from SAP Authorizations perspective, users and roles have to be exported, as first step. Once is PRD copied into TST, these exports have to be imported to enjoy fresh data keeping the same permissions as before systemcopy.

There are more reasons why stay consistent of users and roles, let me briefly name some of them:

- If is SAP GRC AC EAM, ID-based firefightning, implemented, FFID are created only in PRD, where usually gets powerful  permissions. Systemcopy without users&roles export+import creates FFID in TST. Similar for Role-based firefighting. Unwanted garbage, possible security risk that needs to be kicked out.

- If is User Management handled using CUA, systemcopy without users&roles export+import generates inconsistency between master CUA system and "new PRD" as child system.

- If is new/changed role/auth related development/etc. only in TST, being tested or already tested and just waiting for release date, whatever, systemcopy without users&roles export+import makes it never been in TST - overwritten, gone.

Have a good day.

BR, Olga
